leaderRationale: Pushback. Both reviewers rejected with material findings: reviewer-claude reported blocking output-path/evidence-chain failures, and reviewer-codex reported major authority, governance, and lifecycle defects. These are fixable draft/governance issues rather than a non-recoverable blocker, so blocked is not appropriate.
unresolved: Canonical artifact paths are not fully rooted/aligned in <outputs> and contract package_files with tools/agent-role-builder/role/...; self-check still reports the required canonical outputs as missing.

Authority precedence is incorrect: the draft treats static docs as operative authority instead of COO/runtime governance, and the write boundary does not accurately cover run-scoped drafts and evidence artifacts.

Board-governance semantics are invented or overconstrained: the draft requires a Codex leader and unanimous approve semantics instead of the runtime's no-material-pushback freeze rule using approved/conditional outcomes.

Artifact lifecycle is still inconsistent across <steps>, <outputs>, and <completion>; canonical promotion, run-scoped evidence, and per-terminal-state completion are not defined as one authoritative matrix.
<scope> still contains duplicate or contradictory out-of-scope statements, especially around application workflow and runtime orchestration.
Round 0 material findings are not explicitly mapped forward or resolved; there is no evidence chain showing the prior 5 unresolved issues were addressed.
improvementsApplied: All required top-level XML tags are present, and Step 3 explicitly enumerates the required tag set.
The draft separates outputs into canonical, evidence, and internal artifact groups instead of one undifferentiated list.
The board-review flow includes split-verdict handling plus provenance and reviewer-pair guardrails.

conceptual_groups.summary: Authority is anchored to the wrong sources and the write boundary is underspecified.

findings.description: The authority chain says the role is subordinate to `docs/v0/architecture.md` and `docs/VISION.md`, but the bootstrap and architecture sources say the COO controller governs turns and runtime rules come from the memory engine. Treating static docs as operative authority is an authority-model mismatch.

`Filesystem writes outside role package directories` is too narrow for the described workflow, which also produces run-scoped drafts, rounds, runtime state, decision logs, and summaries outside the canonical role directory. The write boundary is therefore unclear and inaccurate.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Define authority in terms of the COO-governed runtime contract first, with static docs as reference evidence only. Then state the write boundary precisely: canonical role package paths plus the current run directory and any explicitly governed support artifacts.

conceptual_groups.summary: Several board-governance rules are invented or stricter than the schema/runtime contract.

findings.description: The role text requires one live Codex leader, but the request schema allows the leader provider to be `codex`, `claude`, or `gemini`; only reviewer pairs are constrained to Codex+Claude. The leader restriction is therefore not grounded in the schema shown.

The freeze rule says every reviewer must approve before freeze, but the actual board contract/runtime uses `approved` or `conditional` as non-blocking and freezes when no blocking or major issues remain. This markdown rule overconstrains the process and conflicts with the contract semantics.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Only state board rules that are actually enforced by the request schema and runtime. Separate reviewer-pair requirements from leader-provider rules, and express freeze criteria in the same materiality terms used by the review contract.

conceptual_groups.summary: Artifact lifecycle is incomplete and internally inconsistent across steps, outputs, and completion.

findings.description: The `<outputs>` section lists generic canonical filenames but does not reference the canonical `required_outputs` paths from the contract summary, which is why the self-check is correctly flagging missing required outputs.

The workflow says `result.json`, decision log, and board summary are written during resolution, but the outputs section omits the run-scoped role markdown, role contract, decision log, and board summary artifacts that exist before freeze and remain evidence-only on non-frozen runs.

Completion criteria mix output-dir completion with `Decision log updated (on freeze)`, which obscures that non-frozen terminal statuses still complete and still need explicit evidence artifacts, while canonical promotion happens only on freeze.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Model lifecycle in three explicit tiers: canonical promotion targets from `required_outputs`, run-scoped evidence artifacts in `output_dir`, and internal audit artifacts. Then align completion rules with all terminal statuses, not just freeze behavior.

residual_risks: Reviewers may reject valid runs because the markdown freeze rule is stricter than the implemented `approved/conditional` semantics.
Future updates may drift further if static docs remain framed as operative authority instead of runtime-governed evidence.
Artifact promotion and non-frozen evidence handling will stay ambiguous until canonical versus run-scoped outputs are explicitly separated.
strengths: All required top-level tags are present, so the package has a usable structural skeleton.
The core mission is clear: governed role-package creation with pushback instead of invented semantics.
The draft already emphasizes provenance, reviewer pairing, and non-freeze when material pushback remains, which is the right overall direction.

conceptual_groups.summary: Output paths are abstract/relative rather than rooted — self-check confirms canonical artifacts are not referenced with their actual filesystem paths

findings.description: Self-check reports all four canonical outputs missing because <outputs> lists slug-prefixed filenames without the canonical directory prefix (tools/agent-role-builder/role/). The contract's package_files section also omits the directory root.

Internal run artifacts (rounds/, runtime/, self-check.json, etc.) are also listed without a rooted path, making it impossible to determine whether they land in the run directory, the role directory, or somewhere else.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: All artifact entries in <outputs> must include their full canonical path rooted at the tool directory (e.g., tools/agent-role-builder/role/<slug>-role.md for canonical, tools/agent-role-builder/runs/<run-id>/rounds/round-<n>.json for internal). The contract package_files values must match exactly.

conceptual_groups.summary: Scope section contains duplicate and contradictory entries, signalling copy-paste drift

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice under Not-in-scope with slightly different wording, and 'Application runtime orchestration' appears twice with conflicting parenthetical qualifiers — once as fully out-of-scope, once as in-scope for board-review.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Deduplicate both entries. Retain one clear statement per concept: application workflow creation is out-of-scope; board-review orchestration *within this tool* is in-scope. Express the distinction in a single sentence rather than two contradictory bullets.

conceptual_groups.summary: Freeze condition is stated in two places with subtly different wording, creating an authoritative-source ambiguity

findings.description: <guardrails> says 'Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader reports no unresolved material issues.' Step 4 repeats this but adds 'Treat any mixed reviewer verdict within a pair or across pairs as non-frozen until explicitly resolved.' These are consistent in intent but the split creates two canonical sources for the freeze rule — any future edit to one that misses the other will introduce a contradiction.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: State the complete freeze condition once, in <guardrails>. In Step 4 replace the repeated freeze rule with a pointer: 'Apply freeze criteria as defined in <guardrails>.'

conceptual_groups.summary: Context-gathering step numbering references are misaligned with the actual steps

findings.description: context-gathering items say '(precondition before Step 1)' and '(Step 2)' but the numbered steps in <steps> begin at Step 1 = 'Validate and normalize'. Preconditions that happen before Step 1 are un-numbered, making the cross-reference ambiguous for an agent parsing the document.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Either number the preconditions as Step 0 in <steps> and reference them as Step 0, or remove the step-number back-references from <context-gathering> and instead say 'performed before the main workflow begins'.

conceptual_groups.summary: Prior round unresolved count (5) is not addressed — no evidence of improvement

findings.description: Round 0 recorded 5 unresolved issues and 0 improvements. The current draft still contains the same structural issues (duplicate scope bullets, missing rooted paths) without any change log or resolution record. This means the revision loop did not execute or its output was not applied.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Before submitting to review again, apply all revisions and attach a changelog entry in the resume package listing each Round 0 issue and how it was addressed. A re-submission with 0 improvements applied must be rejected to prevent infinite loops.

residual_risks: Bootstrap circularity: agent-role-builder creating its own role is inherently self-referential — if the role definition is wrong, the tool that fixes it is also wrong. No external validation gate is defined for this case.

Arbitration trigger condition ('2+ consecutive splits') is mentioned in Step 4 but the arbitration procedure itself is not defined anywhere in the role — an agent executing this step has no instructions for what arbitration actually does.

max_review_rounds=3 combined with allow_single_arbitration_round=true means a run could silently terminate after 4 rounds with material pushback still present if the counter implementation conflates arbitration rounds with review rounds.
strengths: Governance guardrails are substantive: the no-invent-semantics rule and the freeze-requires-no-material-pushback rule are precise and enforceable.
Artifact lifecycle table in <outputs> correctly separates canonical, evidence, and internal run artifacts with clear promotion conditions.
Split-verdict and per-reviewer iteration logic in Step 4 is a real improvement over a single-pass review — it avoids one reviewer blocking the other unnecessarily.
Contract and markdown are structurally aligned on primary_objective and out_of_scope entries.
Self-check step (Step 3) explicitly validates the required XML tag set, which gives the tool a machine-checkable completeness gate before board review.
